dialogue.utterance: Patient secured on stretcher with full spinal board in place and rails upright. Ready for transport.
Patient is in supine, reassessment complete, and ready to roll.
Unable to obtain patient identity or medications; no allergies per ePCR.
Passing off to ED-R bed 2 team; patient is stable with normal saline running via intraosseous line.
Pulses in all extremities noted; minor abrasions to elbows and knees, no motor response documented.
Initial pulse at 109 bpm consistent with tachycardia per ePCR.
All lines in place and functioning; IO line in left humeral head running per KVO.
[UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] noted; no patient signature or ID to pass forward.
No motor response to stimuli and no pain assessment applicable per ePCR.
Passed care to Sarah Hinckley RN in ED-R bed 2.
Initial BP 179/87, stable for transport per documentation.
No adverse events documented during transport.
Full documentation complete and passed to the hospital team.
Eyes are 2 mm non-reactive, with a contusion and abrasion to the left forehead.
No improvement in responsiveness, Glasgow Coma Scale remains 3 with non-reactive pupils.
[UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] passed to hospital staff; no patient identity available.
Patient care and report given to Sarah Hinckley RN upon arrival.
Patient moved via 6-person team lift to ED-R bed 2; no complications.
[COMPANY_NAME] contacting hospital per Paramedic [NAME], no incident during handoff.
Patient moved to ED-R bed 2 with no complications.
No extra movements or delays in the process.
Patient left for transport and arrival as planned.
All equipment functioning throughout the transport journey.
Patient status unchanged at the discharge point.
[COMPANY_NAME] selected per ePCR and dispatch standards.
No new interventions performed beyond airway suction, IV, IO, and assisting ventilation via BVM.
No new medications administered; field glucose levels noted at 160, then 0 post-transport.
Transport consistent with protocol and successful arrival at [COMPANY_NAME].
Protocol 4-1 followed per ePCR for general trauma management.
All care provided per standing orders including ECG monitoring and EC-G settings.
No change in interventions; BVM-assisted ventilation and ECG continued as shown.
Initial SpO2 was 97% and later 94% per documentation.
Documented glucose levels 160 and later declined to 0 post-transport.
Respirations were 41 and later 20; improved after airway clearance.
Field EKG shows sinus tachycardia with four lead monitoring at [COMPANY_NAME].
Intraosseous access established for normal saline.
IV started in extremity vein as second access.
Airway was suctioned twice to maintain clear airway manually.
Ventilation assisted via BVM mask post-suctioning of airway.
No nuchal rigidity or swelling to neck found, trachea midline.
No signs of purpura, rashes, or bite marks observed besides low-intensity abrasions.
No signs of lymphadenopathy or swelling in neck region.
Bilateral lung sounds clear and equal, no abnormalities detected.
Cardiac monitoring showed no abnormal rhythms except for sustained sinus tachycardia.
Abdomen is soft and not tender with no guarding behaviors documented.
No abnormal rhythm detected, heart sounds normal during field monitoring.
Airway confirmed patent post-suctioning and verbalized under no distress.
Shallow respirations initially with no signs of cyanosis upon arrival.
Strong and regular pulse detected at initial check; no drop noted post-intervention.
Oxygen saturation stable throughout field care.
Pulse at 109, consistent with high cardiac output and sympathetic response.
Mottled skin at forehead; otherwise, no other signs of shock or pallor.
Chief complaint confirmed as unconscious after trauma with head injury from vehicle.
Unable to collect patient history due to unconscious state and lack of ID.
Environmental allergies listed as NKA.
No medications noted or collected.
Spinal immobilization applied as per protocol 4-1.
Decerebrate posturing noted but declined with assisted ventilation.
No involvement in pain, given unconscious state of patient.
No motor response per Glasgow Coma Scale, motor score at 1.
No verbal response-the patient is unresponsive.
Patient does not open eyes to any external stimuli.
AVPU check: Alertness-Unresponsive, Voice-no response, Pain-no response, Unresponsive.
Airway deemed patent and self-maintained following suction.
Spine immobilized to help maintain airway.
No signs of respiratory distress or flaring nostrils.
No signs of increased work of breathing after suctioning.
Equal and symmetrical chest movement post-intervention.
Reassessed pulse at 103-mildly elevated but trending stable.
BP at 162/105-elevated but stable for transport.
RespirATORY rate at 20, no signs of distress.
SpO2 at 94% post-transport, minor decline from initial 97%.
Glucose dropped to 0 post-transport; less reliable post ICU stabilization.
Field impression was altered mental status and injury to head, handled per protocol 4-1.
Field interventions unchanged during transport; maintained all lines, assisted breathing.
No new treatments documented per ePCR.
Reassessment showed stable vitals, BP trending down.
IV site assessed for patency.
Continuation of per protocol orders, including chest monitor and oxygenation support.
No medication administered post-EKG check.
Patient ready for ED-R intake with all documentation in hand.
No changes reported en route to destination.
All EMS actions documented and verified for hospital intake.
No signs of decompensation during transport.
All rtakes within acceptable parameters per ePCR.
Cardiac monitoring remained in place for [COMPANY_NAME] upon arrival.
All protocol actions completed and handed to ED.
Patient arrival confirmed; transport officially complete.
Call concluded with successful transport and handoff per EMS protocol.
No missed or undocumented procedures.
Final instructions followed and patient alertness ceilinged.
No rashes or bites seen following full skin sweep.
No signs of increased effort or shallow breathing at reassessment.
Bilateral breath sounds equal and effort consistent post-intervention.
No changes in patient status en route.
Per protocol, destination set at [COMPANY_NAME] upon ED-R bed availability.
Oxygen saturation remains at 94% post-intervention.
Full report passed to ED-R Bed 2.
Paramedic team present for final ED care and transition.
Call complete and ready for hospital transfer.
